_doc: Ground-truth labels for the precision/recall harness. 'expect' lists the ERROR/WARN rule_ids a domain reviewer judges SHOULD fire on each doc. INFO findings are advisory and are not scored as FP/FN (reported separately). Labels are independent of the code's design: where the tool fires an ERROR/WARN not in 'expect', that is a candidate false positive; where an expected rule does not fire, that is a candidate false negative. Profile used: mildoc.

good_cui_opord.md.note: Well-formed CUI OPORD: CUI banners top+bottom, full DI block, all 5 O-SMEAC sections, complete mission/execution. Advisory INFO ok (portion_marking_recommended, orientation_absent).

good_naval_letter.md.note: From/To/Subj in order; Subj uppercase, no terminal punctuation.

good_namp_record.md.note: Complete NAMP/CSEC record with parseable date. Advisory INFO ok (record_shape_ok).

good_warnord.md.note: WARNORD with mission+execution+timeline. Advisory INFO ok (orientation_absent).

plain_memo.md.note: Non-military prose. Noise test: nothing should fire at all.

bad_cui_banner.md.note: Invalid CUI//CTI banner, no DI block, exposed SSN, in CUI context.

bad_namp_missing.md.note: NAMP record missing corrective action, due date, evidence.

bad_opord_missing_cs.md.note: OPORD missing Command and Signal; weak mission and execution.

bad_fragord.md.note: FRAGORD with no base-order reference and omitted sections.

legacy_marking_doc.md.note: FOUO legacy marking + ITAR language without CUI marking.

good_cui_portions.md.note: CUI info paper with consistent portion markings and a full DI block.

good_fragord.md.note: FRAGORD with base-order reference and 'No change' on each section.

good_order_orientation.md.note: OPORD with an Orientation section; should not fire orientation_absent.

good_naval_full.md.note: From/To/Via/Subj/Ref/Encl in order; Subj uppercase, no terminal punctuation.

good_namp_ooma.md.note: Complete OOMA/NALCOMIS NAMP record with parseable date.

order_alias_service_support.md.note: OPORD using 'Service and Support' for paragraph 4; alias must satisfy admin_logistics.

cui_in_prose.md.note: Open bulletin ABOUT the CUI program, not a CUI document. Probes whether mentioning 'controlled unclassified information' in prose over-triggers the banner/DI checks.

maintenance_prose.md.note: Break-room notice with two maintenance words. Probes whether namp over-triggers on ordinary prose.

casual_mission_prose.md.note: Values statement using mission/situation/execution casually with no order hint; osmeac must stay silent.

phone_numbers_doc.md.note: Fabricated phone numbers; pii.ssn must not match phone formats.

ten_digit_not_edipi.md.note: 10-digit tracking/barcode numbers with no EDIPI context; pii.edipi must not fire.

bad_cui_unclass_prefix.md.note: UNCLASSIFIED//CUI banner in CUI context, no DI block.

bad_pii_edipi_dob.md.note: EDIPI in context and a date-of-birth field.

bad_naval_subject_case.md.note: Subj line lowercase and ending in a period.

bad_warnord_sparse.md.note: WARNORD with only a Situation paragraph.

bad_namp_unparseable_date.md.note: Complete NAMP record but due date is 'sometime next week'.

itar_marked_cui.md.note: Properly marked CUI doc that mentions ITAR; possible_unmarked_category must NOT fire because CUI marking is present (precision check).

empty.md.note: Empty document: must not crash, must not fire.

ssn_lookalike.md.note: SSN-shaped stock number in a supply doc. Domain-clean. Probes pii.ssn precision (high-recall regex is expected to over-fire here).
